title: Business News
description: India is catching up fast with US, China in AI race: IndiaAI CEO Abhishek SinghIn an exclusive interview following the Grand AI India Impact Summit, Abhishek Singh, CEO of India's Mission AI, discusses the nation's emerging role in the global artificial intelligence landscape.19:22

title: India is catching up fast with US, China in AI race: IndiaAI CEO Abhishek Singh
description: In an exclusive interview following the Grand AI India Impact Summit, Abhishek Singh, CEO of India’s Mission AI, discusses the nation's emerging role in the global artificial intelligence landscape. Singh highlights three major takeaways: India's placement on the global AI map, the 'Digital India moment' for youth and startups, and the surge in global investments. Addressing the 'Galgotia robo-dog' controversy, Singh clarifies, 'It was more about the wrong claim that was made, the misleading claim that was made, rather than what was exhibited,' noting that the stall was cancelled after the firm falsely claimed the product as indigenous. He emphasizes that India is 'catching up fast' with the US and China through models like Sarvam and Bharat GPT. Singh also outlines the 'Manav' approach, focusing on human-centric, inclusive AI that prioritises Indian languages and service delivery in healthcare and agriculture. He remains optimistic about India's potential to become a global hub for AI transformation and chip design within the next few years.

title: At 9:19 am, the BSE Sensex slipped 88.05 points, or 0.11%, to 82,410.09, after falling as much as 291 points in early trade.
